title: Nal Sarovar Bird Sanctuary
tagline: Nal Sarovar Bird Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Gujarat

overview.short: Nal Sarovar Bird Sanctuary, consisting primarily of a 120.82-square-kilometre (46.65 sq mi) lake and surrounding marshes, is situated about 64 km to the west of Ahmedabad near Sana
overview.description: Nal Sarovar Bird Sanctuary, consisting primarily of a 120.82-square-kilometre (46.65 sq mi) lake and surrounding marshes, is situated about 64 km to the west of Ahmedabad near Sanand Village, in the Indian state of Gujarat. Mainly inhabited by migratory birds in winter and spring, it is the largest wetland bird sanctuary in Gujarat, and one of the largest in India. It was declared a bird sanctuary in April 1969. The lake attracts over 210 species of birds in the winter, and harbors a variety of plants, fish, and animals.

topPlaces.description: Ranagadh is a small village in the Limbdi tehsil of Surendranagar district in the state of Gujarat in India. It is one of the villages in Gujarat where malnutrition claims lives of several children.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nal Sarovar Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nal Sarovar Bird Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nal Sarovar Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad (70 km) and the nearest railway station is Ahmedabad Junction (65 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nal Sarovar Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nal Sarovar Bird Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nal Sarovar Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Nal Sarovar Bird Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nal Sarovar Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ranagadh, Gujarat.
